mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based demand destruction, forcing earnings downgrades and negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annually during inflationary periods confirms supply-side resilience, supporting corporate margins and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of high-inflation periods or loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL falls below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates aggressive real rate tightening, which rapidly suppresses equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >65% of wide-spread observations or the interaction effect vanishes in rolling window analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls drives consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >50% of positive payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% annually signals economic stagnation, prompting equity market repricing and negative monthly forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of low-growth quarters or the threshold effect is not statistically significant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle confirms supply constraints are worsening, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal yields positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances or loses predictive accuracy when federal funds rate remains unchanged.
